concerns.text: Tasks T020, T021, and T022 (Modeling) are listed as parallel-safe [P] or independent, but they all consume the output of T015 (Processed Data with derived 'condition'). T015 is in Phase 3 (US1), while T020-022 are in Phase 4 (US2). The ordering implies US2 can start before US1 is complete, violating the 'Producer before Consumer' rule. US2 explicitly depends on the 'condition' column derived in US1.

Tasks T029 (Threshold Sweep) and T030 (Bootstrap) in Phase 5 (US3) are marked [P] or independent, but they depend on the model outputs generated in Phase 4 (T024). The 'Dependencies & Execution Order' section correctly notes US3 depends on US2, but the task list structure (grouping by phase) and [P] tags on T029/T030 create a semantic contradiction where a consumer task appears parallel-safe to its producer.

The 'Checkpoint' comments at the end of each phase imply sequential completion ('At this point, User Story 1 should be fully functional...'), yet the 'Parallel Opportunities' section claims 'Once Foundational phase completes, all user stories can start in parallel'. This is a semantic conflict: US2 and US3 cannot start in parallel with US1 because they consume artifacts (processed data, model outputs) produced by US1 and US2 respectively. The [P] tags on implementation tasks across stories are misleading.
